document.docAuthor: Singh, Ankit, Singh, Harsh & Nautiyal, Mohan Chandra
document.docTitle: Polygonatum tungnathensis Ankit Singh, Harsh Singh & M. C. Nautiyal 2022, sp. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: Polygonatum tungnathensis (Asparagaceae), a new species from Uttarakhand, Western Himalaya, India

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Polygonatum tungnathensis (Asparagaceae), a new species from Uttarakhand, Western Himalaya, India

mods:name.mods:affiliation: High Altitude Plant Physiology Research Centre (HAPPRC), H. N. B. Garhwal University, Post Box 14, Srinagar, Garhwal, 246174, Uttarakhand, INDIA & ankitsinghrawat 333 @ gmail. com; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0003 - 3769 - 685 X

Department of Botany, Centre for Advanced Studies in Botany, North-Eastern Hill University, Shilong- 793022, Meghalaya, INDIA & harshchamlegi @ gmail. com; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0002 - 9416 - 895 X

High Altitude Plant Physiology Research Centre (HAPPRC), H. N. B. Garhwal University, Post Box 14, Srinagar, Garhwal, 246174, Uttarakhand, INDIA & mcnautiyal @ gmail. com; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0002 - 8369 - 2350

paragraph.heading:
Polygonatum tungnathensis Ankit Singh, Harsh Singh & M.C. Nautiyal
sp. nov.
( Fig. 2 )

subSubSection.paragraph:
Type :— INDIA , Uttarakhand , Tungnath ,
30 ° 29ʹ33.88ʹʹ N
,
79 ° 12ʹ58.03ʹʹ E
,
3330 m
,
15 July 2020
, Ankit Singh Rawat 202145 ( holotype GHU!; isotypes: BSD!)
.

caption.paragraph:
TABLE 1. Comparative morphology along with its SEM micro-morphology of
Polygonatum verticillatum
and P.

tr.td: Linear to narrowly lanceolate, leathery lustrous adaxially
Cuticular ridges obscure, irregular cuticle surface
Cuticular ridges conspisious (longitudinally elongated and transversely narrowed) and rectangular cuticle surface
Orange or yellowish; Ffb145, ffebb2, f2ab53
Whitish yellow; e8d380, e3d17d, ecde97, ead8a8
Dense anticlinal wall with raised cells
Scattered anticlinal wall with smooth sunken cells
With ruminant of bractlet and occasionally with leafy bractlet
Immature fruit green with black strip in the middle of carpel
Immature fruit yellowish with purple or red blotches

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 2.
Polygonatum tungnathensis
sp nov. A —Habit, B —Phyllotaxis, C —Leaf adaxial, D —Leaf abaxial, E —Juvenile stem with stipule, F —Stem, G —Root, H —Inflorescence, I —Bractlet, J —Flowers front view with anther arrangement, K —Single flower with lateral view, L —Open perianth and stamen, M —Pedicel with ruminant of bractlet, N —Ovary and style, O —Immature fruit with purple or reddish blotches, P —Mature fruit, Q —Seeds. (Photo: Ankit Singh)

FIGURE 3.
Polygonatum verticillatum
. A —Habit, B —Phyllotaxis, C —Leaf adaxial, D —Leaf abaxial, E —Juvenile stem with stipule, F —Stem, G —Root, H —Inflorescence, I —Flowers front view with anther arrangement, J, K — Open perianth and stamen, L —Immature fruit with black line and blotches, M —Mature fruit, N —Seeds. (Photo: Ankit Singh)

FIGURE 4. Leaf abaxial surface. ( A , C , E ) Anticlinal wall pattern and papillae of
Polygonatum tungnathensis
. ( B , D , F )
P. verticillatum .
( A , C , B , D ) Papillate vs non papillate surface of cuticle. ( E , F ) Small outgrowth scattered vs densely ornamented.

subSubSection.paragraph:
SEM micromorphology :—SEM micromorphology of
P. tungnathensis
significantly differ with
P. verticillatum ,
especially in foliar surface ornamentation. Abaxial leaf surface papillate vs non papillate ( Figs. 4 A, C, B, D ) small outgrowth scattered vs densely ornamented ( Figs. 4 E, F ). Leaf adaxial cuticular ridges conspicuous (longitudinally elongated and transversely narrowed) vs obscure ( Figs. 5 A, B ) rectangular cuticle surface vs irregular surface ( Figs. 5 C, D ) dense outgrowth vs scattered ( Figs.5 E, F ), ruminant of bractlet are small and resemblance like minute thorn ( Figs. 6 ). Filament with dense semi oval shaped having striate surface of papillae vs filament with sharply pointed papillae ( Figs. 7 (A, B). Non papillate filament vs papillate filament, ( Figs. 7 A, C, B, D ) rugulate-perforate vs smooth surface of flower outer side ( Figs. 7 E, F ). Larger vs smaller pollen grains ( Figs. 8 A, B ), tricolpate vs monosulcate (pollen type ) ( Figs. 8 C, D ), colpus extended almost towards the grain end pollen ornamentation reticulate; the murus is psilate ( Figs. 8 E, F ). In
P. tungnathensis
the seed ornamentation is irregular shaped pavement cells vs conspicuous rectangular shape of pavement cells in
P. verticillatum
( Figs. 9 C, D ), and granulate vs smooth surface ornamentation of seeds ( Figs. 9 E, F ).

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 5. Leaf adaxial surface. ( A , C , E )
Polygonatum tungnathensis
. ( B , D , F )
P. verticillatum
. ( A , B ) Cuticular ridges conspisious (longitudinally elongated and transversely narrowed) vs obscure. ( C , D ) Rectangular cuticle surface vs irregular surface. ( E , F ) Dense outgrowth vs scattered.

FIGURE 6. Pedicle pubescence. ( A , C ,) Ruminants of bractlet
Polygonatum tungnathensis
. ( B , D )
P. verticillatum
. ( A , C , B , D ) cuticular ridges with conspisious papillate vs obscure or non papilate.

,

,

(BSD); Tehri Garhwal, Kalyani,

,

,

3500 m
,
14 Aug 1978
,
A. K . Goel
, 64471 (BSD); Tali,
4300 m
,
21 May 1979
,
A. K . Goel 66625
(BSD); Jamnotri ,
18 June 1965
,
B.P. Shetty & J.P. Sharma 33195
(BSD); Pauri Garhwal , Dobri forest ,
10 May 1995
,
B.P. Uniyal 90675
(BSD); Chamoli , Duggalbhitta P.W.D. R.H. ,
2300 m
,
23 May 1985
,
R.R. Rao 76245
(BSD)
.

Etymology :—The specific epithet ‘
tungnathensis
’ is derived from the type locality Tungnath, Uttarakhand .
Distribution :— 3000–4000 m from treeline to subalpine zone of the Western Himalaya.

Conservation status:—
Polygonatum tungnathensis
occurs in small fragmented population in gentle grassy and rocky slopes. Populations are severely affected by intense grazing, unscientific harvesting for its medicinal uses, habitat reduction and irregular tourism activity. The species assessed as Vulnerable on the basis of extent of occurrence (EOO) and area of occupancy (AOO) (B1B2a) and number of mature individuals (C2ai,D1).

Habitat and ecology :—Usually erect or arching herb associated with
Tenaxia cachemyriana ( Jaubert & Spach 1851:331 ) Barker & Linder (2010:352)
,
Rhododendron campanulatum Don David. (1821:410)
and
Impatiens sulcata Wallich (1824:458)
.
